title: Tyrn Jiton
content:

Jiton is stated to be 27 years old in the book, meaning he must have been born in [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]].</ref>

{{Quote|…the face value of a given bounty is the least important factor in the selection process. The key to any successful hunt lies in the selection of the proper target.|Tyrn Jiton|<ref name="GG">''[[Galaxy Guide 10: Bounty Hunters]]''</ref>}}
'''Tyrn Jiton''' was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] [[Devaronian/Legends|Devaronian]] who operated as a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] within the [[Trans-Hydian/Legends|Borderland Regions]] of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] during the first [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]] in order to fund his [[Gambling/Legends|gambling]] expenses. Jiton also perpetrated kidnappings, selling his victims to either the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] or [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lords]]. For this reason, he ran afoul of the [[New Republic Intelligence Service]], who put out a warrant for his arrest as soon as they discovered his nefarious activities.

==Biography==
{{Quote|In the Guild, oddballs are the norm, not the exception. Any may the Edge bless us for harboring such a stable of extraordinary freaks. Take Tyrn Jiton, so precise with a blaster rifle he only needs to fire it once, but so reckless with gambling that he's lost seven fortunes.|Arack Dostryt, Bounty Hunters Guild Handbook|<ref name="TBHC" />}}
[[File:Tyrn_Jiton_GG10.jpg|thumb|left|175px|Tyrn Jiton in a bar]]
Tyrn Jiton, a [[Devaronian/Legends|Devaronian]] [[Gender/Legends|male]], was born on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Fenves]]<ref name="WC" /> in [[20 BBY/Legends|20 BBY]].<ref name="WC Dates" /> Having expensive tastes as an adult—including [[Gambling/Legends|gambling]]—he became a [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunter]] during the [[Time/Legends|time]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] so that he could earn enough money to satisfy his penchants.<ref name="GG" /> Jiton affiliated himself with the [[Bounty Hunters' Guild/Legends|Bounty Hunters' Guild]], an ancient institution that dominated the [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] hunting profession,<ref name="TBHC" /> and as a hunter, he was known to be selective about the targets he tracked. At some point, the tip of his left horn was severed.<ref name="GG" /> Jiton's taste for gambling led to loosing fortunes of wealth on seven different occasions, which earned him a reputation within the Bounty Hunters' Guild as an oddball and he received a mention in the [[Bounty Hunters Guild Handbook|Guilds' handbook]], in a passage by the hunter [[Arack Dostryt]].<ref name="TBHC" />

After the creation of the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]]<ref name="WC" /> in [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="NR">[[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']]</ref> Jiton was active in the [[Trans-Hydian/Legends|Borderland Regions]], an area of [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] bordering both Imperial and Republic [[Realspace/Legends|space]]. During this time, he kidnapped [[Operative|agents]] of the New Republic and sold them to the Empire. Jiton also kidnapped other individuals to sell to either the Empire or [[Crime lord/Legends|crime lords]], achieving remarkable success at this task. The New Republic did know that a bounty hunter was behind these kidnappings, but they were unaware of the hunter's identity until they captured an Imperial [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]].<ref name="WC" />

[[General/Legends|General]] [[Airen Cracken/Legends|Airen Cracken]] of the [[New Republic Intelligence Service]] then composed a detailed report on Jiton. He also issued a warrant for his arrest on charges of theft, kidnapping and murder, and offered a 12,000 [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credit]] bounty on Jiton. Following the issuing of Cracken's report, Jiton [[Pilot/Legends|piloted]] a [[Firespray-31-class patrol and attack craft/Legends|''Firespray-31''-class patrol and attack craft]] and was considered dangerous.<ref name="WC" /> By [[7 ABY/Legends|7 ABY]],<ref name="WC Dates" /> the Devaronian had escaped from New Republic agents and was last seen in the [[Gantonnerre system/Legends|Gantonnerre system]] before disappearing from Cracken's sights.<ref name="WC" />

==Personality and traits==
Jiton used his bounty hunting career as a means to support his lifestyle of high-stakes gambling and jamboree, which commonly included illegal activities. When hunting, he preferred stealth and ambush over direct confrontation with his target, to the point that his prey was frequently unaware of Jiton's presence. Whenever Jiton was asked to [[Death/Legends|kill]], he took pride in using only one shot to eliminate the target.<ref name="GG" />

Jiton was a non-[[Force-sensitive/Legends|Force-sensitive]]<ref name="GG" /> Devaronian who could be identified because he had lost the tip of his left horn. He had honed several of his skills, including tracking, repairing technology, and piloting. He was also a proficient brawler.<ref name="WC" /><ref name="GG" /> At some point between the period between [[0 ABY/Legends|0 ABY]] and 4 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|ABY]]<ref name="GG10 note">''[[Galaxy Guide 10: Bounty Hunters]]'', which includes [[Malindin Gevarak]]'s [[Bounty/Legends|bounty]] posting, focuses on [[Bounty hunter/Legends|bounty hunting]] during the [[Time/Legends|time]] period between the events of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']] and [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']], which [[The Essential Chronology (real-world book)|''The Essential Chronology'']] places in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]] and [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]] respectively.</ref> and 7 ABY,<ref name="WC Dates" /> he also increased his ability to hide.<ref name="WC" /><ref name="GG" />

==Equipment==
During the years of the Empire, Jiton used both a [[Blaster pistol/Legends|blaster pistol]] and a [[Blaster rifle/Legends|blaster rifle]], as well as [[Grenade/Legends|grenades]], a [[Thermal detonator/Legends|thermal detonator]], a [[Knife/Legends|combat knife]], and a [[neurostaff]]. He wore partial [[Armor/Legends|armor]] and carried with him [[Syntherope/Legends|syntherope]] and a [[Comlink/Legends|comlink]]. His gear also included [[MCI-100 magnacuffs/Legends|MCI-100 magnacuffs]] and a [[MCI-200]] magnaharness,<ref name="GG" /> but he stopped using those two items after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].<ref name="WC" />

==Behind the scenes==
Tyrn Jiton was first mentioned in [[Wanted by Cracken (sourcebook)|''Wanted by Cracken'']], a [[1993]] [[Roleplaying game|role playing]] sourcebook for [[West End Games]]' ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'', which detailed enemies of the New Republic. The book was written by [[Louis J. Prosperi]] and included an image of Jiton drawn by [[Rob Caswell]].<ref name="WC" /> The [[1994]] role playing sourcebook ''[[Galaxy Guide 10: Bounty Hunters]]'' included a new image of Jiton by [[David Plunkett]], and updated role-playing game statistics that specified his skills.<ref name="GG" /> However, the changes from ''Wanted by Cracken''<ref name="WC" /> to ''Galaxy Guide 10'' reduced his ability to parry brawling attacks,<ref name="GG" /> something very uncommon following the rules of the role-playing game.<ref name="RPG">[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game (first edition)|''Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game'']] specifies that skills commonly will increase with time; this is exemplified in other books covering characters through different stages of their lives such as ''[[Galaxy Guide 1: A New Hope]], [[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back]]'' and ''[[Galaxy Guide 5: Return of the Jedi]]''.</ref>
